title: What Copilot is becoming
excerpt: In 2021, when I first told people I was building the AI assistant in Outlook, the response was a polite variation of "interesting, what does it do."

title: Samsung, Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, Microsoft again
excerpt: The thing nobody tells you about changing companies is that you keep arriving as a beginner.

title: Why I came back
excerpt: The conversation with the Microsoft team started in November 2024, with a message from my former skip-level, who was now running a broader organization and wanted to talk about the Copilot work.

title: The authentication problem in a virtual room
excerpt: When you put on a VR headset, the familiar anchor points disappear.

title: What it is like to be an IC again
excerpt: The first week of code I wrote at Meta was an authentication middleware layer in Kotlin, and I wrote it alone, on a MacBook Pro that Meta had shipped to the house, sitting at the desk in the home office at about eleven in the morning on a Thursday in February 2024.

title: The difference between Microsoft and Meta
excerpt: The thing you notice first at Meta, coming from Microsoft, is the pace of the document culture.

title: The first Copilot feature nobody saw
excerpt: We shipped a voice assistant into Outlook in 2021 that almost no one used.

title: Why I left Microsoft for Meta
excerpt: The honest version of why I left Microsoft in late 2023 is not the version I gave in exit interviews.

title: What enterprise software is actually like to build
excerpt: The first customer advisory board I attended was in 2022, a room in a downtown Seattle hotel where twelve IT administrators from large companies sat at a conference table and told us, politely, about the ways our product had made their lives complicated.

title: Cross-team dependencies
excerpt: In the spring of 2023, there was a team whose timeline controlled ours.

title: Writing a performance review
excerpt: The Microsoft performance cycle ends in August, and for the three years I was a manager, I started writing reviews in late July, in the hour before the family calls and after the evening's meetings.

title: The ChatGPT moment from inside Microsoft
excerpt: ChatGPT launched on a Wednesday in November 2022, and by Friday my calendar had three new recurring meetings on it.

title: What I got wrong about being a manager the first time
excerpt: The first team I managed had four people on it.

title: Being inside a company when it decides to change direction
excerpt: In early 2020, we were building a voice assistant for Outlook. Nobody called it an AI feature yet.

title: The product that almost shipped
excerpt: We called it Compose, though that was an internal name and it never got an external one, because it never shipped.

title: Letting someone go
excerpt: The conversation had been six weeks in preparation.

title: The year I stopped writing code
excerpt: Somewhere in my second month as a manager, I opened my laptop on a Sunday morning with the intention of writing a small script.

title: The first team meeting as a manager
excerpt: On September 22, 2021, I ran my first team meeting as the official manager of the M365 Copilot mobile experiences team.

title: The first hire
excerpt: His name was Karan, and he was a senior engineer at a mid-sized company in Seattle who had been referred to me by someone on the team.

title: Shadowing my manager
excerpt: From February to September of 2021, I sat in on my manager David's one-on-ones with the engineers on his team.

title: What a six-pager teaches you about thinking
excerpt: When I joined Microsoft in March 2020, I brought the six-pager habit the way you bring a coffee order to a new city, assuming it will be readable somewhere.

title: Joining Microsoft during Covid
excerpt: My start date at Microsoft was March 16, 2020.

title: What it felt like to ship to five million people
excerpt: The dashboard loaded the same way it always did, slow on the first refresh, faster on the second.

title: The day we hit a million
excerpt: It was a Thursday morning in January 2020, just before I was planning to leave Amazon, and the dashboard was showing just over one million active users for Alexa Hands-Free on Android in India.

title: Building voice on a phone with bad signal
excerpt: The assumption baked into every voice assistant I had ever used was that the connection was good.

title: Amazon's writing culture
excerpt: The six-pager is six pages of dense prose, no bullets, no slide decks, no executive summary at the top because the whole document is its own executive summary.

title: What Amazon's writing culture taught me
excerpt: The six-pager is a document you write instead of making slides.

title: The hardware partner call
excerpt: The call came on a Friday afternoon in October 2019, which is the wrong time for a call of this kind.

title: Building for India from Seattle
excerpt: The apartment in Fremont where I lived in 2019 had a kitchen window that faced east, and some mornings I would stand there with coffee and a phone and be on a call with the partner team in Hyderabad while the light came up over Lake Union.

title: What Alexa doesn't understand
excerpt: In late 2018, the most common failure mode in Alexa on Android was what the team called a false wake.

title: The first week at Amazon
excerpt: I started at Amazon on a Tuesday in late June 2018, because they had moved my start date back two weeks for onboarding logistics and I had spent those two weeks in my Bellevue apartment reading every public Alexa paper I could find.

title: What research actually is
excerpt: My second year at USC I worked in the lab of a professor who was studying natural language understanding for low-resource languages.

title: The internship that changed direction
excerpt: The summer between my first and second year at USC, I worked at a company in Santa Monica that was building speech interfaces for enterprise software.

title: Los Angeles as a city
excerpt: The first thing you notice in Los Angeles, if you've spent your whole life in Indian cities, is the absence.

title: Going back to school at twenty-four
excerpt: The professor who taught the distributed systems course was already halfway through the syllabus when I realized that the students around me were twenty-one and asking questions I had trained myself out of asking.

title: The patent that started as a side project
excerpt: It started because phone notifications were bothering me.

title: Leaving India for the first time
excerpt: My flight was on a Saturday evening in early August, Emirates via Dubai, landing at LAX sometime Sunday local time.

title: Building something nobody asked for
excerpt: The notification problem started bothering me in my own life before it became anything else.

title: What a patent filing actually looks like
excerpt: The lawyer's name was Kiran, and he worked for a firm in Chennai that Samsung retained for IP filings.

title: The first day at Samsung
excerpt: The Samsung R&D campus in Noida was larger than I had expected, which is a strange thing to notice when you are twenty-two years old and mostly just trying not to look lost.
